taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete statements about the lymphatic system.
textField:
The *thoracic* duct drains lymph from the entire body except the right side of the head, neck, and right upper limb, which drain via the *right lymphatic* duct.

Approximately *3* litres of fluid per day leaks from blood capillaries into the interstitium and must be returned by the lymphatic system.

Specialised lymphatic capillaries in intestinal villi called *lacteals* absorb dietary fats as *chylomicrons*.

Lymphatic capillaries have overlapping endothelial cells and a *discontinuous* basement membrane, unlike blood capillaries.

Secondary lymphoedema in coastal Tamil Nadu is commonly caused by *Wuchereria bancrofti* infection, leading to *elephantiasis*.

The thoracic duct begins as the *cisterna chyli* at the level of L1-L2 and drains into the junction of the left *subclavian* and internal jugular veins.

Lymph nodes filter lymph and contain *B lymphocytes* in the cortex and *T lymphocytes* in the paracortex.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review lymphatic system features and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent understanding of the lymphatic system!
